title: Crepidium trichopodum
taxonomy: Crepidium trichopodum is classified under the kingdom Plantae, with its more specific classification being part of the family Orchidaceae. This is a family of flowering plants commonly known as orchids. Within Orchidaceae, Crepidium trichopodum is further classified under the subfamily Epidendroideae. It is also classified as a genus within the subtribe Spiranthinae. Further, Crepidium trichopodum is a species within the genus Crepidium.

The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various health conditions. intro: Crepidium trichopodum, also known as the "Horned Pleurothallis," is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Crepidium trichopodum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Crepidium trichopodum is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies such as infusions and decoctions.
